mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE drops > 0.2pp month-over-month, or predictive R-squared falls below 0.01 over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising residential mortgage debt indicates synchronized credit expansion and construction activity, which amplifies wealth effects and lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any rolling 12-month period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index above 0.8% demonstrates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns within one month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns average below zero when INDPRO exceeds the 0.8% threshold, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 across three distinct market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0% annualized rate confirms underlying economic momentum, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity returns over a three-month forward window.
failure_condition: The sign of the GDP growth coefficient flips to negative in a rolling 5-year window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between high-growth and low-growth quarters (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading that exceeds the 3.0% year-over-year threshold triggers inflation hedging demands and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits no predictive power for volatility spikes (VIX forward change < 0.05) during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing employment cycle, which reduces recession probabilities and lifts equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive risk-adjusted returns relative to a market-cap weighted benchmark over any 24-month rolling window, or the correlation between the two series breaks down during stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts consistently exceeding 1400 thousand units signals sustained residential investment and construction sector strength, which drives positive spillover effects to related industrial and financial equities over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns average below the risk-free rate when HOUST breaches the 1400 threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 10-year backtest.
